title: Episode 11
wikitext:
[]: Episode 11...             Quote Post by Kotono-Chawan » Sun Apr 22, 2007 2:51 pm I was watching episode 11, The Day Tokyo-3 stood Still, last night and after all the times I have watched that episode something grabbed me. Ritsuko says they're performing the second prolonged EVa activation test, but Rei is at school with Asuka and Shingi while the test is being performed. How is this possible? this is before the dummy plugs and Rei clones are known so it wouldn't make sense to say it's a dummy plug test... I'm just confused because doesn't and EVA need a pilot to activate? So why isn't Rei there?

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Sun Apr 22, 2007 3:00 pm I never noticed that.

[]:             Quote Post by Szmitten » Sun Apr 22, 2007 3:09 pm I assumed that the test was going to take place after school, hence why the Children were making their way to NERV in the first place.

[]:             Quote Post by Kotono-Chawan » Sun Apr 22, 2007 3:13 pm I used to assume that too, but the test has already started while the children are making their way there. Ritsuko says to abort the test and some nerve up link is bad. This is just before Shinji calls his father.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sun Apr 22, 2007 3:29 pm While on the train: ep11 plat subs wrote: RITSUKO: Yes, we're going to run the second prolonged activation test at 10:30.

[]:             Quote Post by Kotono-Chawan » Sun Apr 22, 2007 3:48 pm That makes sense. I'm gonna say that the writers just didn't think it through that well now :o

[]:             Quote Post by Zkh » Sun Apr 22, 2007 5:27 pm While we're on the subject of episode 11, every time I watch it, something feels wrong... Something with the character designs looks off to me, but in the episodes after that they were back to normal, what the hell is up with that?

[]:             Quote Post by Space Penis » Sun Apr 22, 2007 6:07 pm Zkh wrote: While we're on the subject of episode 11, every time I watch it, something feels wrong... Something with the character designs looks off to me, but in the episodes after that they were back to normal, what the hell is up with that?

[]:             Quote Post by Big Panda » Sun Apr 22, 2007 6:10 pm Zkh wrote: While we're on the subject of episode 11, every time I watch it, something feels wrong... Something with the character designs looks off to me, but in the episodes after that they were back to normal, what the hell is up with that?

[]:             Quote Post by Kaysow » Mon Apr 23, 2007 9:39 am #10 & #11 are the ugliest episodes. I'm partial #9 and #16, led by Shinya Hasegawa.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Apr 23, 2007 10:05 am Zkh wrote: While we're on the subject of episode 11, every time I watch it, something feels wrong... Something with the character designs looks off to me, but in the episodes after that they were back to normal, what the hell is up with that?

[]:             Quote Post by Executor » Mon Apr 23, 2007 6:29 pm Big Panda wrote: Zkh wrote: While we're on the subject of episode 11, every time I watch it, something feels wrong... Something with the character designs looks off to me, but in the episodes after that they were back to normal, what the hell is up with that?

[]:             Quote Post by V » Tue Apr 24, 2007 12:24 am Yes, Asuka just looks....OFF in episode 11.

[]:             Quote Post by Kotono-Chawan » Tue Apr 24, 2007 8:16 am I rewatched episode 11 last night then went onto the next disc and it was jarring! Pleasantly jarring, but everything just shifted. It was weird.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Apr 24, 2007 8:26 am I actually like the look of episode 11... :(

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Tue Apr 24, 2007 9:23 am V wrote: Yes, Asuka just looks....OFF in episode 11.

[]:             Quote Post by Ayana-Me » Tue Apr 24, 2007 11:31 am Yeah, Asuka's face looked different while they were crawling through the ventilation ducts. Overall though, it doesn't hold a candle to Rei in EoE when she and Shinji are naked in the LCL together. I don't know what they were thinking, but that did not look like her at all.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Apr 24, 2007 4:33 pm Asuka's face espeically looked somewhat off, but even in "bad animation" days of Eva, I still think it looks similar, it doesn't look THAT off in the sense where you can't even tell if its the same character or not. *cough* Badly animated=EoE Kyoko-sama. Maybe her being killed wasn't so horrible...she was put out of her fugly-looking misery that way.

[]:             Quote Post by Kotono-Chawan » Tue Apr 24, 2007 9:31 pm Episodes 9-11 seemed more anime-y in a way, episode five as well. Misato just looked weird in that one. It's like eyes were more round, a little more shimmery, the animation reminded me of Gundam or Ranma. It wasn't as realistic as I've become accustomed too. I just got the DVDs two weeks ago so I got used to the movie animation which is quite different from the TV animation.

[]:             Quote Post by Big Panda » Tue Apr 24, 2007 9:32 pm I guess that episode had the more... voluminous Asuka. That's a good point. And "Asuka-on-duct" really had a decent face.

How is this possible? this is before the dummy plugs and Rei c
